comment.body:
To the ones shorting early, hope you have better luck on monday. I tried doing it on Wednesday towards close with spy 0DTE puts with the hope Thursday data drop on unit labor costs might shock the market. I had to cut my losses and only sized small since it was a gamble to begin with.

My advice don't try to front run this, it is quite possible Monday is another ripper and if CPI does not deliver on Tuesday will go even higher.

Alma's weekly post on Sunday might throw some additional light on this however market falling next week is only one among multiple possibilities:) could rip higher as well.

comment.body:
As far as I remember, after the tariff crash Alma said April Opex would be supportive, and her main trades were shorting volatility. The risk was rather high as the "weighing machine" seemed switched on.

Then, Bessent managed to mask the bear market via stealth QE. Mechanical flows, TACOs and squeezes held the market up aggressively. Alma also said that the market should be supportive until late July~ early August, when tariff effects might show in data.

user.name: Jeffrey K
user.bio: my market is good

comment.body:
IMO unlikely to be a significant drawdown without shift in narrative (bad news is bad news).  If CPI were to much hotter Tuesday then maybe we have a Sept ‘22 CPI release kind of day…market  up 1% PM into the print and then close down 3% followed by a 10% drawdown into Sep.   Given that 35% of CPI price data is now derived not actual pricing data, I’m not sure reliable it is anyway and likely understated just like the jobs data.  

Long end of the yield have to blow out, which only happens with higher inflation risk, bonds don’t seem to care about term premia atm.  The decelerating jobs numbers counteract driving yields lower and the Fed will have to choose which mandate to address.  They will choose jobs and cut, however inflation will return and long end yields will still rise imo, but that is 2026 forward, which will impact equities.

The geopolitical black swan events are being managed as Trump will fold to 99% of events that could upset the status quo.  I think China invading Taiwan is the only real possible black swan, all the rest is priced in.

comment.body: In T/A it's a good way the ascending (or descending) wedges can continue trending, when a massive reversal isn't called for yet. Just drop price action, (even one trade to mark the chart,) down to the level where you make it into a channel, and continue the original wedge trend. Useful when a rarer 'breakout squeeze' can't be managed . Nice Technical Analysis trick to be aware of, so we (I 😆) don't get locked into reversal bias, which I'm prone to. Makes a channel out of the ascending/descending wedge.
comment.raw_body: In T/A it's a good way the ascending (or descending) wedges can continue trending, when a massive reversal isn't called for yet. Just drop price action, (even one trade to mark the chart,) down to the level where you make it into a channel, and continue the original wedge trend.
